title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s are frustrating, especially when permissions haven't changed.

To help narrow this down: when you get the 403, what are you doing at that moment?

- **A.** Simply loading/refreshing the main dashboard page
- **B.** Clicking into a specific task or project within the dashboard
- **C.** Performing an action (creating, editing, or deleting something)
- **D.** Something else

Also — when it happens, does refreshing the page fix it, or does the 403 persist for a while?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: It's mostly A — just loading or refreshing the dashboard. But honestly it happens on pretty much everything, like if I click into a project I get it too. The weird thing is if I just refresh the page it usually works again. Sometimes I have to refresh a couple times but it goes away. I've been assuming it's some kind of network issue on my end but my internet seems fine otherwise.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for the detail — the pattern of intermittent 403s across your whole team that clear on refresh strongly suggests something changed on the server/infrastructure side rather than a network issue on your end.

Do you know if anything changed around the time this started — a couple days ago? For example:

- **A.** A new deployment or release went out
- **B.** Changes to authentication/SSO configuration
- **C.** Infrastructure changes (new load balancer, CDN, reverse proxy, etc.)
- **D.** Not sure — I don't have visibility into that

If D, no worries — knowing approximately when it started (even a specific date) would help the team correlate with deploy logs.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I think it was A — I heard there was some kind of deployment a couple days ago. Something about roles or permissions being updated? I was recently switched to a new 'analyst' role around the same time, but I figured that was unrelated since the role change went through fine. I'm not sure about the infrastructure details though, I don't really have visibility into that stuff.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
This is starting to come together. Here's what I'm thinking:

The recent deployment updated the role/permissions system, and around that same time you were moved to the new 'analyst' role. The intermittent nature — 403 one moment, fine after a refresh — suggests a **caching issue** with authorization decisions (e.g., stale session data, a cache that sometimes serves old permission checks, or a race condition in the new role lookup).

One thing that would really pin this down: the teammates who are also seeing 403s — were they also moved to the 'analyst' role, or are they on different roles?

- **A.** They were also switched to the 'analyst' role
- **B.** They're on different roles (and still getting 403s)
- **C.** Not sure what roles they're on

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I think they were also switched to the analyst role — at least the ones I've talked to about it. We're all on the same team and we all got moved over around the same time. I haven't heard anyone on other teams complaining about it, but I also haven't really asked around broadly. Come to think of it, my manager is still on the old role and she said she hasn't had any issues.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden errors for users on new 'analyst' role after recent permissions deployment","problem":"Users who were recently migrated to the new 'analyst' role are experiencing intermittent 403 Forbidden errors across all dashboard pages. The errors are non-deterministic — the same page may return 403 on one request and load successfully on the next refresh. Users on the old role are not affected. The issue began immediately after a deployment that updated the role/permissions system.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The recent deployment introduced the 'analyst' role but likely has a caching inconsistency in the authorization layer. Possible causes: (1) a permissions cache that intermittently serves stale entries that don't include the new role, (2) a race condition where the role lookup resolves before the new role's permissions are fully loaded, or (3) multiple app servers/containers where some have stale permission mappings for the new role. The fact that a refresh usually fixes it suggests a short-lived cache TTL or per-request cache miss pattern.","reproduction_steps":["Ensure a user account is assigned the new 'analyst' role","Navigate to the TaskFlow dashboard","Refresh the page repeatedly (may take several attempts)","Observe that some requests return 403 Forbidden while others succeed","Compare with a user on the old role — they should never see 403s"],"environment":"Production environment, post-deployment from ~2 days ago (approx. April 5, 2026). Affects multiple users on the 'analyst' role across the same team.","severity":"high","impact":"All users migrated to the new 'analyst' role are affected. They can work around it by refreshing, but it disrupts workflow and erodes trust in the platform. As more users are migrated to the new role, the blast radius will grow.","recommended_fix":"1. Check the authorization/permissions cache layer for how the 'analyst' role is resolved — look for stale cache entries or inconsistent cache invalidation after the deployment. 2. Check if multiple app server instances have different permission configurations (rolling deployment that didn't fully propagate). 3. Review the role lookup code path for race conditions where the role is recognized but its permissions aren't yet loaded. 4. As an immediate mitigation, consider flushing the permissions cache or forcing a re-deploy to ensure all instances have consistent role definitions.","proposed_test_case":"Create an integration test that assigns a user the 'analyst' role and makes 50+ sequential authenticated requests to the dashboard endpoint, asserting that all return 200. Run this against a deployment that includes role/permission changes to catch intermittent authorization failures.","information_gaps":["Exact deployment identifier/timestamp to correlate with deploy logs","Whether the authorization system uses an explicit cache (Redis, in-memory) vs. session-based checks","Server-side error logs or access logs showing the 403 responses for correlation"]}
